column1: Comparative Table: PIE Myth Motifs vs. Quranic Parallels
Key Findings:
The Quran actively rejects PIE-like triads (e.g., trinity, three goddesses) but acknowledges their cultural influence.
Trials in the Quran are tests of faith, not heroic quests (e.g., Abraham’s sacrifice, Moses’ confrontations).
No true "three sisters" motif exists except as condemned idols (53:19-23).
Chaos battles are eschatological (Gog & Magog) rather than god-vs-monster myths.
